story.iWant: to copy prompts, responses, and code blocks to clipboard
story.soThat: I can reuse content in other applications or modify prompts
story.tasks: - Add Copy button to user messages (copy to input box) - Add Copy Response button to assistant messages (copy to system clipboard) - Add Copy Code button to code blocks (copy code only, no backticks) - Implement clipboard service via IPC bridge (Electron) - Implement format preference persistence (text vs markdown) - Add toast notifications for copy actions - Add keyboard shortcut support (Ctrl+C/Cmd+C)

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: Copy button on user messages copies content to input box and focuses it (TM §6.1)
Copy Response button on assistant messages copies to system clipboard (TM §6.2)
Copy Code button on code blocks copies code only, no backticks (TM §6.3)
Copy Response supports text and markdown format options (TM §6.4)
Keyboard shortcut Ctrl+C/Cmd+C works on selected messages

doc.title: Epic 2 Technical Specification
doc.snippet: ClipboardService: copyToInput(messageId), copyToClipboard(content, format: 'text' | 'markdown'), copyCodeBlock(code, language), handlePaste(event). Uses Electron clipboard API via IPC.

doc.title: Epic 2 Technical Specification
doc.snippet: Clipboard operations maintain desktop-first architecture with fallback to web clipboard API. Use Electron's secure clipboard API via IPC bridge.

doc.title: Epic 2 Technical Specification
doc.snippet: Input Sanitization: Clipboard paste operations sanitize HTML/scripts before insertion (XSS prevention). Reject executable files, scripts, and unknown MIME types.

artifact.reason: NEW - Copy to input button for user messages
NEW - Copy to clipboard button for assistant messages with format dropdown
Existing component - needs Copy Code button overlay
NEW - Renderer wrapper for clipboard IPC calls
NEW - Main process clipboard service using Electron API
NEW - Strip markdown formatting for text format option

story-context.constraints: - **Desktop Implementation:** Electron clipboard API in main process. Renderer uses IPC bridge for security. - **IPC Pattern:** Renderer invokes 'clipboard:write' → Main handles → clipboard.writeText(). No direct clipboard access from renderer. - **Format Options:** Text format strips markdown (**, __, `, links). Markdown format preserves formatting. - **Keyboard Shortcuts:** Ctrl+C / Cmd+C on focused message. Don't interfere with text selection within message. - **Toast Notifications:** Different messages: "Copied to input", "Copied to clipboard", "Code copied". Auto-dismiss after 2 seconds. - **Dependency on E4-S1:** NotificationService for toast notifications.

interface.name: ClipboardService.copyToInput()
interface.signature: copyToInput(messageContent: string): void - Sets input field value to message content, focuses input, scrolls into view

interface.name: ClipboardService.copyToClipboard()
interface.signature: copyToClipboard(content: string, format: 'text' | 'markdown'): Promise<void> - Copies to system clipboard via IPC, strips markdown if format='text'

interface.name: stripMarkdown()
interface.signature: stripMarkdown(text: string): string - Removes markdown formatting (**, __, `, links, headers) from text

tests.standards: Use vitest for unit tests. Test markdown stripping with various inputs. Test copy format preference persistence. Test IPC clipboard write communication. Test toast notifications appear on copy. Test keyboard shortcut triggers copy.

test.idea: Unit test: Click Copy on user message, verify input value set to message content, input focused
Unit test: Click Copy Response with format='text', verify markdown stripped, clipboard contains plain text
Unit test: Click Copy Response with format='markdown', verify markdown preserved in clipboard
Unit test: Click Copy Code on ```python code```, verify clipboard contains 'code' only (no backticks, no language)
Integration test: Copy action → verify toast appears with correct message, auto-dismisses after 2s
Integration test: Set format preference to 'text', restart app, verify preference remembered
Unit test: Focus message, press Ctrl+C (Windows) or Cmd+C (Mac), verify copy triggered
Unit test: stripMarkdown('**bold** and __italic__') returns 'bold and italic'
